ab: Maqtu aak mapabazbaz suu?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Uu, Azuq.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: Maqtu aak mapabazbaz suu?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Nii, Nii nak madia qansaipun.
en: B:Don't, I don't know much about it.

ab: Haiza suu tinaungqu ail-uan? Na asa aak mun su-uan tantungu maqtu aas?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I'd like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: Azuq, Na ihan aak inak lumaq mantatala. su’u.
en: B: Okay, I'll wait for you at home.

ab: Haiza suu laqbingin ail-uan? Na asa aak mapabazbaz suu pali’uni itu Bunun minpakaliva ituqabasan tu liniqaiban.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於布農族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I would like to ask you about the legendary story of the Bunun tribe.

ab: Laqbingin a na muhan aak ba’av, Na uka aak han lumaq.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I'm going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won't be home.

ab: Tinan-ah aak tu madau aas a pali-uni itu Bunun iniqumisan tu dailaz maqansiapdaingaz Na asa mapasnava suu.
ch: A:我聽說您對布農族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you know a lot about Bunun traditional customs and would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: Nii nak madia qansaipun, Qaal na mintamasaz aak antalam suu.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don't know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: Maqtu aak siqaningu su’u? Maqi huata tauna haiphaipin ti na mahaipi upatasan.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: Uu, Azuq
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: Tupa suu tu piaq in suu qamisan?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: Masivaun qan vau in nak qamisan.
en: B: I'm 98 years old.

ab: Talmaindu sadu’an.
en: A: You're so young.

ab: Ni aau! Madaingazin aak.
en: B: No, I'm not! I'm old.

ab: Tupa suu tu maina isaq aas?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: Maina han aak Bahuan asang minsuma.
en: B: I come from the Mayon tribe.

ab: Tupa suu tu Bunun suu pakatuszangan?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是布農族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also a Bunun?

ab: Nii, Put aipa.
en: B: No, she's a Han Chinese.

ab: Maqansiap aas Mama’aq qalinga?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: Maqansiap aak MalasPut siin malasBunun.
ch: B: 我會說漢語和布農語。
en: B: I speak Chinese and Bueno.

ab: Haip qanian manaskal mapabazbaz suu, Tanama taqu, Ma pali-uni qanup tu samuh maqtu?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It's a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: Uu ki! Ma talmaindu ang aak a qanglas mun han ba’av qanup.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: Uninang! Ma haip sinpasnava suu mananu tu masihaldaingaz.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: Ma nak sintaqu mamantukdaingaz, Isia maimadadaingaz sinpala’ tu sintaqu.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: Haip tu sinpabazbaz na pan-iti in. Uninang tutuza isu tu sinngunu tu sipaibazbaz.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That's all for today's interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: Nitu kua, Na muqna ata mapabazbaz.
en: B: You're welcome, let's talk again.

ab: Na mudanin aak. Mantalah muqnin tan-a madadaingaz tu liniqaiban tu sintaquh.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I'm going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: Uu ki! Daudauk naip mudandan.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
